ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class; no specific entry for honey peach flavoured RTD tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for honey peach flavoured less-sugar RTD tea. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged honey peach flavoured tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle of packaged honey peach tea is approximately 500 ml (500 g), providing around 180 kcal per bottle.

nutrient_source.title: Honey Peach Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Honey Peach Tea (Less Sugar, packaged), cr_id D09020047. All per-100g values preserved as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific packaged honey peach tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 52 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages and fructose-glucose sugar blends (honey-based). Honey has a GI of approximately 55–58; diluted sweetened tea beverages with mixed sugars typically fall in the low-to-medium range (GI 45–58). A conservative estimate of 52 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 45.45) / 100 ≈ 23.6 for a 500 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.09 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium (5 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage: despite its very low sodium content, the 9.09 g/100 g sugar load classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), and SSB consumption is independently associated with elevated blood pressure (AHA 2018); plain water or unsweetened tea are strongly preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.09 g sugar/100 g and 0 g dietary fibre; liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed, causing acute postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this packaged honey peach tea: the 9.09 g/100 g sugar content with zero dietary fibre and an estimated GI of 52 delivers approximately 45 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per 500 ml bottle, producing a glycemic load of ~23.6 per serving, which is clinically significant for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation, insulin resistance, and inflammatory pathways; this beverage contains 9.09 g sugar/100 g with no protective nutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage: habitual sugar-sweetened beverage intake is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased LDL-C, and systemic inflammation; the absence of fibre, omega-3 fatty acids, or any cardioprotective nutrient provides no offsetting benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 9.09 g/100 g with zero fibre and no anti-inflammatory components promotes pro-inflammatory mediators relevant to RA disease activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage: the high sugar content promotes inflammatory cytokine activity, and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or whole-food fibre means there is no anti-inflammatory offset; unsweetened green or herbal teas are preferred alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Peach Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a commercially produced ready-to-drink (RTD) tea beverage sold in Singapore, flavoured with peach and sweetened with honey and added sugar in a reduced-sugar formulation. Despite the 'less sugar' label, the product contains 9.09 g of sugar per 100 ml, which classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and WHO definitions. The beverage is made from water, tea extract, peach flavouring, and honey, and is typically sold in 500 ml bottles. It is ultra-processed, vegan-unfriendly (honey), halal-suitable, and gluten-free. Nutritionally, it provides 36 kcal per 100 ml, with virtually all energy derived from carbohydrates (9.09 g/100 ml), of which all are sugars. Protein and fat are absent by formulation. Sodium is very low at 5 mg/100 ml. Dietary fibre is zero. The estimated glycemic index is 52 (low category), but the glycemic load per 500 ml serving is approximately 23.6, which is clinically significant. This beverage is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its SSB classification and high sugar load. It is also a concern for cardiovascular health with regular consumption. It is commonly consumed as a refreshment beverage at breakfast or throughout the day in Singapore and is widely available in convenience stores, supermarkets, and vending machines.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics drink packaged peach tea?
How much sugar is in honey peach tea?

Calories in bottled honey peach tea Singapore

What is the GI of packaged peach tea?
Is honey peach tea a sugar-sweetened beverage?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020047, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from this primary approved source. GI is an analogous estimate documented with approved GI source reference, reducing overall confidence for GI only; core nutritional confidence remains high.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by formulation — this is a water-based sweetened tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this RTD tea product. saturated_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged RTD tea product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D09020047 as of 2026-04-16. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened tea beverages and honey-based sugar blends; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (52 × 45.45) / 100 ≈ 23.6. The 'less sugar' label is a relative claim compared to the standard formulation; the product still qualifies as an SSB under HPB and WHO criteria (>5 g sugar/100 ml). Vegan flag set to false as honey is an animal-derived ingredient.
